tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58293,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware and new Responses logging features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary); also `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58274 added the following features to the AgentServer packages: 1. **`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`** (Core, `beta.22`) — a new auto-registered ASP.NET Core middleware that logs every inbound HTTP request with method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`, OpenTelemetry trace ID). Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available. 3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag. ## Gaps Found - The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. It only says traces come from the Responses/Invocations activity sources, which is now incomplete and misleading — users may not realize all HTTP requests are automatically logged. - The **Core README Key concepts section** does not list `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` or describe the built-in HTTP-level logging as a default server behavior. - The **Responses README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`) does not mention handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, leaving users unable to discover what log categories/levels to configure. - The **Responses README Key concepts section** does not document `agent_session_id` derivation — developers building conversation-aware handlers need to understand that session IDs are deterministically derived from agent name, version, and partition hint, and what the fallback behavior is. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Health endpoint`: ```markdown ### Request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically and logs every inbound HTTP request to the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` logger category: | Event | Log level | Fields | |-------|-----------|--------| | Request started | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `StatusCode`, `DurationMs`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request failed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | Same as above | Query strings are intentionally excluded from all log messages to avoid logging sensitive data. To adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware": "Warning" } } } ``` ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured `Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for 4xx/5xx responses. Correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) and the OpenTelemetry trace ID are included in every log entry for cross-service request tracing. The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Session ID derivation Each response is assigned a deterministic `agent_session_id` derived from a SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the request. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead. The derivation priority is: 1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available) 4. Random 63-char hex (fallback) ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. Each log entry includes the `ResponseId` and, where applicable, `ConversationId`, `PreviousResponseId`, `Model`, `Streaming`, `Background`, `Store`, `Status`, and `OutputCount` fields: | Endpoint | Log details | |----------|-------------| | POST `/responses` | response ID, model, streaming, background, store, conversation ID, previous response ID | | POST `/responses` (completed, non-streaming) | final status and output count | | GET `/responses/{id}` | retrieved status and output count | | POST `/responses/{id}/cancel` | result status | | DELETE `/responses/{id}` | success flag | | GET `/responses/{id}/input_items` | response ID | The `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. OpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose storage and handler issues. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for these documentation-only additions. All content is conceptual description and configuration examples that do not need snippet extraction. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs new subsections added** as described in Step 1 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **Logging subsections need updating** as described in Step 1 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 6. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` No snippet update or API export is needed since these are documentation-only changes. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24571508680/agentic_workflow) · ● 339.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58293","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T15:05:15Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T15:05:15Z"}

Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available. 3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag. ## Gaps Found - The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. To adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware": "Warning" } } } ``` ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured `Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for 4xx/5xx responses. The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. The partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the request. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead. The derivation priority is: 1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available) 4. Random 63-char hex (fallback) ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. OpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the README files for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` following PR #58274, which added `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation, and handler-level structured logging but did not update the corresponding documentation sections.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (1.0.0-beta.22), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.2)
**Affected API:** `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, `agent_session_id` derivation, handler-level structured logging, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`
**Scenarios:**
- Core README Key Concepts and Troubleshooting/Logging sections are missing documentation of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` and its automatic registration
- Responses README Key Concepts section is missing documentation of deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation logic and fallback behavior
- Responses README Troubleshooting/Logging section does not describe handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`

**Root ask:** Update both READMEs to document newly added logging features and session ID derivation so developers can discover and configure these behaviors.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap (no runtime bug). The implementation guide in the issue provides exact Markdown edits for both README files including tables, configuration examples, and fallback priority lists.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58274 (commit 48e9f7bd) to confirm the actual behavior of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` and `agent_session_id` derivation matches the documentation proposed in the issue body
2. Apply the edits described in the Implementation Guide to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` and `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`
3. Verify no existing tests or snippets reference the old Logging section text; run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` for both packages to confirm no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — These are data-plane ASP.NET Core server-side SDK packages (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.*`), not management plane or provisioning libraries
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The packages reside in `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — The service directory `sdk/agentserver/` is unambiguous, the category is clearly data-plane client, and the CODEOWNERS mapping is direct
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the issue concerns `sdk/agentserver/` packages which map directly to the `%AI Agents` label
